summary: Audience‑recorded May 12 1977 show shines with stellar Half‑Step/Mississippi, Dancin', and Samson jams, but suffers pitch glitches and missing Cassidy.
review: The performance is a high‑water mark for the spring ’77 tour – Jerry’s soaring solo on Jack Straw, a monster Half‑Step > Mississippi sequence, a ripping Dancin’, and a ferocious Samson & Delilah keep fans raving.  The audience (AUD) source captures the venue vibe but is marred by speed/pitch problems in the second set, noticeable hiss, and the loss of “Cassidy”, which drags the technical rating down.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is consistently praised (≈4.5/5) while recording quality is mixed (≈3.0/5). Weighted combination (70 % show, 30 % recording) yields ~4.1 stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audible high‑end presence and decent ambience, but suffers from pitch/speed issues (especially late NFA), hiss at low levels, drums a bit recessed, and the complete loss of “Cassidy”.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is solid and representative of the May ’77 vibe; second set builds into marathon jams (Half‑Step/Mississippi, Dancin', Samson) that many describe as “wild, wooly, and fun” despite the technical hiccups.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His solo on Jack Straw is repeatedly called transcendent and a highlight of the night.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as sounding like “two angels”, with no complaints noted.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano/organ work (and Donna’s vocal contributions) are praised, especially on Sunrise and Brown‑Eyed Women.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drum presence is debated – some note they’re a bit low or muffled, others say the rhythm section stays tight throughout.
